instruction: Apply the rubric to the candidate response and return the verdict.

inquiry.subject: Case #88 — delayed parts shipment
inquiry.body: Hi, our case #88 was filed Monday about the delayed parts shipment. We still haven't heard back. The parts are needed for a production line that's currently idled. Could you give us an update?

candidate_response.subject: Re: Case #80
candidate_response.body: Hi, thanks for following up on case #80. We're looking into the delayed shipment with our logistics team and will get back to you shortly. We understand this is impacting your operations and appreciate your patience. Best regards, Support.

rationale_hint: Two failures: C2 ('shortly' no ETA) and C5 (cites case #80 vs the customer's case #88). C1, C3, C4 pass. Sits exactly at the 2-fail end of the NEEDS_REVISION band — one more failure would tip into FAIL. Tests whether the judge respects the count boundary.

provenance.source_benchmark: WONDERBREAD SOP Ranking and Demo Validation (Wornow et al., 2024); Kourani et al. (2025) self-improvement; TheAgentCompany feedback subtasks (Xu et al., 2024)
provenance.adaptation: Author-constructed inquiry / candidate-response pair and rubric; task semantics follow the LLM-as-Judge pattern of the cited benchmarks. No benchmark text or data is reproduced. Sub-class: boundary_needs_revision_two_fails.
provenance.license: Original work by the author (rubric, inquiries, candidate responses); benchmark semantics reference only
